body:
### Problem

`:h ]m` provides the following documentation:

```
						*]m*
]m			Go to [count] next start of a method (for Java or
			similar structured language).  When not before the
			start of a method, jump to the start or end of the
			class.  |exclusive| motion.
						*]M*
]M			Go to [count] next end of a method (for Java or
			similar structured language).  When not before the end
			of a method, jump to the start or end of the class.
			|exclusive| motion.
						*[m*
[m			Go to [count] previous start of a method (for Java or
			similar structured language).  When not after the
			start of a method, jump to the start or end of the
			class.  When no '{' is found before the cursor this is
			an error.  |exclusive| motion.
						*[M*
[M			Go to [count] previous end of a method (for Java or
			similar structured language).  When not after the
			end of a method, jump to the start or end of the
			class.  When no '}' is found before the cursor this is
			an error.  |exclusive| motion.

The above four commands assume that the file contains a class with methods.
The class definition is surrounded in '{' and '}'.  Each method in the class
is also surrounded with '{' and '}'.  This applies to the Java language.
```

However, these mappings are fairly limited in functionality: they do not work in C++ files with namespace declarations, or Rust files with submodules, etc.

### Expected behavior

Similarly to how some mappings are overriden when an LSP is attached to the current buffer (e.g., `:h K-lsp-default`), it would be great if the `[m`, `]m`, `[M`, `]M` were also overridden with treesitter support when treesitter is enabled for the current buffer.

In particular, these mappings could be overridden to next / previous start / end of `@function.outer` (or `@function.inner`). Looking at the [compatibility table](https://github.com/nvim-treesitter/nvim-treesitter-textobjects/blob/main/BUILTIN_TEXTOBJECTS.md), this seems to be supported by virtually all languages that have the concept of functions.

Unlike other text object improvements (e.g., https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/22740), the proposal here does not change the semantics or meaning of the builtin text object, and shouldn't pose any surprises or go against existing muscle memory for users.

comment_data.body:
I have this remapped as well and it makes a lot of sense. I also have `im`/`am` mapped for selection and it's useful for grabbing anonymous function args in Lua.

But I'm not sure how this could be upstreamed without the rest of Treesitter Text Objects. I'm fine with having it as a remap if there's not a good answer to that question.

title: Override [m, ]m, [M, ]M builtin mappings when treesitter is enabled

type.description: A request, idea, or new functionality
type.name: Enhancement
